prediction.summary: An attacker from IP 10.20.8.77 successfully uploaded a PHP web shell to the `/uploads/shell.php` path on the web server. The attacker then used the web shell to execute commands, including `whoami`, `cat /etc/passwd`, and attempted to download a secondary payload from `evil.tld`. This activity was detected by WAF, IDS, and SIEM, indicating a critical compromise with remote code execution.

timeline.event: Attacker uploads `shell.php` to `/uploads/shell.php` via POST request.
WAF alerts on suspicious PHP upload to webroot /uploads/shell.php from 10.20.8.77.
Attacker executes `whoami` command via the uploaded web shell.
Attacker executes `cat /etc/passwd` command via the uploaded web shell.
Attacker attempts to download `stage2` from `http://evil.tld/stage2` via the web shell.
IDS detects web shell activity: repeated POST to .php with cmd parameter.
SIEM classifies activity as MITRE T1505.003 Server Software Component: Web Shell.
ModSecurity blocks SQLi probe from 10.20.8.77 targeting the web shell URI.
SIEM issues CRITICAL alert for webshell on host webserver01 from 10.20.8.77.

recommendations: Immediately isolate the compromised host (webserver01) to prevent further lateral movement or data exfiltration.
Perform a full forensic analysis of the web server to identify the initial compromise vector, extent of compromise, and any persistence mechanisms.
Remove the `/uploads/shell.php` file and any other malicious files identified during forensics.
Block the source IP `10.20.8.77` at the perimeter firewall and review logs for other activity from this IP.
Review web application logs for other suspicious activity from the same source IP or similar attack patterns.
Update web application security controls (WAF rules, input validation, file upload restrictions) to prevent similar uploads and command execution vulnerabilities.
